pattern: Merging a second sorted array into the tail slack of the first is done by writing from the back, so already-placed values are never overwritten and no extra storage is needed.
hints: The first array has exactly enough trailing space for the second, and the largest of all values belongs at the very end.
Filling from the front would clobber values not yet compared, so place values starting from the last slot and work backward.

Walk two read pointers from the ends of the real data in each array and one write pointer from the end of nums1, copying the larger of the two compared values each step; once the second array is exhausted the first is already in place.

edge_inputs.description: interleaved merge with trailing zeros as slack
second array empty leaves first untouched
first array empty so result is the second
all of nums2 smaller than all of nums1
all of nums2 larger than all of nums1
single slot from each side, second is smaller

stress.description: two independently sorted random arrays merged into the padded first
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    cap = max(1, min(size, 100))
    m = rng.randint(0, cap)
    n = rng.randint(0, cap)
    if m + n == 0:
        m = 1
    a = sorted(rng.randint(-1000, 1000) for _ in range(m))
    b = sorted(rng.randint(-1000, 1000) for _ in range(n))
    nums1 = a + [0] * n
    return [nums1, m, b, n]
stress.note: varies m and n independently including zero on either side; first array padded with n zero slots
